data.title: UK's Pantheon Infrastructure 2025 NAV rises 10%
data.description:
Overview

  • UK infrastructure investor's NAV rose to £611 mln for 2025, up from £553 mln in 2024

  • Dividend per share increased 3.5% to 4.346p for the year

  • Company completed first realisation with sale of Calpine and invested in Intersect Power

Outlook

  • Pantheon Infrastructure targets a NAV total return per share of 8-10% per annum

  • Company says near-term market conditions remain uncertain and valuation volatility likely to persist

  • Pantheon Infrastructure sees structural tailwinds from digitalisation and energy transition supporting long-term investment opportunities

Result Drivers

  • PORTFOLIO GAINS - Valuation uplifts in assets like Vantage Data Centers and CyrusOne, driven by demand for data centres and AI-related capacity, contributed to performance

  • CAPITAL RECYCLING - Sale of Calpine and partial realisation of Intersect Power generated returns and enabled reinvestment

  • ASSET DIVERSIFICATION - Weaknesses in some assets, such as Cartier Energy and Delta Fiber, were offset by strength elsewhere in the portfolio

Company press release:

Key Details

Metric

FY Net Asset Value

Beat/Miss

Actual

GBP 611 mln

Consensus Estimate

Analyst Coverage

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "strong buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 3 "strong buy" or "buy", no "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell"

  • The average consensus recommendation for the closed end funds peer group is "buy."

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Pantheon Infrastructure PLC is GBp129.50, about 22.7% above its March 30 closing price of GBp105.50

  • The stock recently traded at 6 times the next 12-month earnings vs. a P/E of 5 three months ago

data.title: UK's Venture Life FY results lifted by Power Brands, acquisitions; says 2026 rev ahead of 2025
data.description:
Overview

  • UK consumer healthcare firm's yearly revenue grew 32.2%, driven by Power Brands and acquisitions

  • Adjusted EPS for the year rose 15.4%, reflecting improved profitability post-divestment

  • Company launched share buyback in Sept, returning £1.1 mln to shareholders by period end

Outlook

  • Company says revenues currently trading about 18% ahead of prior year with improved gross margin

  • Venture Life is pursuing M&A opportunities to drive growth in targeted sectors and geographies

  • Company expects profitability initiatives and new product launches to support future revenue growth

Result Drivers

  • POWER BRANDS GROWTH - Revenue growth was driven by strong performance of Power Brands, with Balance Activ, Lift, Earol, and Health & Her/Him all posting gains, especially in the UK

  • HEALTH & HER ACQUISITION - Integration of Health & Her contributed significant revenue and EBITDA growth, with the brand delivering 44% proforma revenue growth and expanding distribution

  • HIGHER OPERATING COSTS - Operating expenses rose due to increased advertising and promotional spend and inclusion of costs from the Health & Her acquisition, impacting EBITDA margin

data.title: Big Tech's $635 billion AI spending faces energy shock test, S&P Global says
data.description:
By Rocky Swift

Massive investments in artificial intelligence that underpinned record runs in equities face a major hurdle as the Middle East crisis clouds prospects for growth and energy costs, said Melissa Otto, head of research at S&P Global Visible Alpha.

Before the Iran war broke out, tech giants Microsoft, Amazon, Alphabet and Meta planned to spend about $635 billion on data centres, chips, and other AI infrastructure in 2026, S&P Global has said.

That figure was up from $383 billion the prior year and just $80 billion in 2019.

Although tech companies have yet to signal cutbacks in those capital investments, persistently high oil prices could force spending revisions in the first and second quarters, bringing a "really meaningful correction in all equity markets," Otto said.

"I think if the capex numbers get pulled back, if in fact energy prices are not reflected in earnings, that could be a catalyst," she added in an interview in Tokyo on Monday.

Euphoria over AI had carried global stock indexes beyond the highs of 2025, with bright hopes for the trend to run further, but it has lost steam since the conflict.

At the same time, energy costs are becoming a constraint.

Data centres require vast amounts of electricity, making the AI dependent on power prices and infrastructure capacity.

At the CERAWeek energy conference in Houston last week, oil executives warned supply risks are not fully reflected in prices, Otto said, raising concerns about further increases with ripple effects for the global economy.

"We're seeing this big question around global growth," Otto added. "Because if you have energy prices jumping 30%, that's going to hurt consumers, that's going to hurt companies."

data.title: Raspberry Pi reports better-than-expected 25% rise in full-year earnings
data.description:
Single-board computing company Raspberry Pi reported a better-than-expected 25% rise in annual adjusted core earnings to $46.4 million on Tuesday, helped by strengthening demand and favourable unit economics in the second half.

The British company shipped 4 million units in the second half, giving a total of 7.6 million for the year, up 7% on 2024.

It said strong sales momentum had carried into the opening months of this year, but it cautioned that second-half visibility was limited by constraints in the DRAM market.

data.title: Korean Won Weakens to 17-Year Low Amid Mounting Growth Concerns — Market Talk
data.description: The Korean won weakens to a 17-year low against the dollar in Asian session amid mounting growth concerns spurred by the Middle East conflict. "Investors [are] placing greater weight on slowing growth rather than renewed inflation pressures from the energy shock," two FX strategists at OCBC Group Research say in a research report. The current round of selling is hitting pro-cyclical currencies such as the won, the strategists say. "There is a gradual shift in repricing of global growth risks as Iran conflict appears to last longer than expected, moving past a volatility event," they add. The dollar rises 1.1% to 1,534.40 won after earlier touching 1,536.80 won, its highest intraday level since 2009, LSEG data show.(ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: Bank of Korea Governor Nominee Says Dollar Liquidity Remains Ample
data.description:
By Jihye Lee

Bank of Korea governor nominee Shin Hyun-song said U.S. dollar liquidity in South Korea remains ample despite recent volatility in the won, suggesting external risks to the financial system are contained.

Speaking to reporters ahead of a parliamentary confirmation hearing on Tuesday, Shin said robust foreign inflows into the local bond market, particularly through foreign-exchange swaps, have helped underpin dollar funding conditions.

"Although the exchange rate is high, dollar liquidity is currently quite ample," he said. "In such cases, investors effectively lend dollars and borrow foreign currency, which means dollar funding is abundant."

"Stronger dollar liquidity means there is less need to link exchange-rate movements with financial-market stability as in the past," he said.

His comments come as the Korean won has come under pressure amid geopolitical uncertainties. Still, Shin, who is expected to assume the position of the head of the central bank mid-April, signaled confidence that domestic markets are well positioned to absorb volatility in the currency.

"The market is capable of absorbing risks related to exchange-rate fluctuations," he said.

The dollar was recently up 1.2% against the won at 1,535.30, according to LSEG data, marking the local currency's weakest level since 2009.

Write to Jihye Lee at jihye.lee@wsj.com

data.title: UK's Flexion Mobile Q4 revenue falls 4% as influencer marketing weakens
data.description:
Overview

  • UK mobile games distributor's Q4 revenue fell 4% yr/yr as influencer marketing weakened

  • Distribution revenue grew 6% yr/yr, offsetting some decline in total revenue

  • Adjusted EBITDA turned positive, helped by cost controls and restructuring

Outlook

  • Flexion expects restructuring to conclude in Q3 2026, aiming for predictable profitability

  • Company sees market changes fuelling demand for services, citing Epic-Google settlement

  • Flexion anticipates improved efficiency as teams introduce AI and streamline processes

Result Drivers

  • DISTRIBUTION GROWTH - Core distribution revenue rose 6% yr/yr, driven by new game signings and growth in gaming distribution customers, per chairman Andrew Shen

  • MARKETING SERVICES DECLINE - Marketing services revenue fell 53% yr/yr due to weak influencer marketing and strategic realignment, including phasing out non-gaming operations

  • COST CONTROLS - Company said cost structure is trending down as core distribution revenue grows, aided by internal efficiency measures and restructuring

data.title: India's Zetwerk files confidentially for IPO draft papers
data.description:
India's Zetwerk has filed confidential draft papers with the market regulator for its initial public offering, a newspaper advertisement on Tuesday showed.

Earlier this month, Reuters reported that the contract electronics manufacturer was aiming to raise up to $550 million at a valuation of about $4 billion.

The company is targeting a listing later this year, the sources said, adding that the IPO includes about $300 million in fresh equity, with the remainder coming from an offer for sale.

In India, a confidential filing allows companies to submit draft IPO papers to the Securities and Exchange Board of India for review without making them public until they decide to proceed with the offering.

India was the world's second‑largest IPO market in 2025, LSEG data showed, but sentiment has weakened this year after a selloff in technology services stocks, with recent listings such as Fractal Analytics and Shadowfax Technologies struggling on debut.

Founded in 2018, Zetwerk supplies industries ranging from consumer electronics to aerospace and defence, with more than 10 facilities across India, the United States, Mexico and Europe. Its client base includes Samsung, Volvo and Honeywell.

data.title: UK's A G Barr yearly revenue slightly beats estimates on higher pricing, raises dividend
data.description:
Overview

  • Adjusted pretax profit for the year slightly exceeded analyst expectations

Outlook

  • A G Barr expects low double-digit percentage revenue growth in FY26/27

data.title: UK's Raspberry Pi 2025 revenue rises 25% on strong demand
data.description:
Overview

  • UK computing hardware maker's 2025 revenue rose 25% on higher unit sales and ASPs

  • Adjusted EBITDA up 25%, driven by demand and improved unit economics

  • Adjusted EPS for 2025 grew 35% yr/yr

Outlook

  • Raspberry Pi expects FY 2026 profitability to be in-line with market estimates, revenue materially higher

  • Company sees strong sales momentum continuing into opening months of FY 2026

  • Raspberry Pi expects DRAM supply constraints to persist beyond this year, with mitigation from demand elasticity and foundry investment

Result Drivers

  • HIGHER-VALUE PRODUCT MIX - Growth in sales of higher-priced Raspberry Pi 4 and 5 boards, along with increased accessory sales, contributed to revenue and profit gains

  • STRONG DEMAND IN KEY MARKETS - Robust demand from OEMs and resellers, particularly in the US and China, supported higher unit shipments

  • SUPPLY CHAIN AND PRICING ACTIONS - Supplier diversification, pricing adjustments, and inventory buffers helped offset rising DRAM costs
